summary: Strong second‑set performance with a smoking Scarlet>Fire and epic Morning Dew, captured on an unknown‑source recording of solid but unverified quality
review: Reviewers rave about the 1995 show’s soaring second set – highlights include a blistering Scarlet > Fire, a stellar All Too Much, deep Supplication jams and one of the most intense closing Morning Dew jams ever. The band’s playing is praised (especially Jerry’s guitar and vocals, Phil’s bass on Broken Arrow, and Bill & Mickey’s drumming), while the first set is noted as merely adequate; no concrete comments about recording fidelity were offered.

ai_rating.rationale: Both detailed reviews give high marks to the musical performance, especially the second set, while offering no criticism of the audio source; weighting favors show quality, yielding a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewers commented on soundboard vs. audience or clarity, so recording quality cannot be determined from the available feedback.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as bland, but the second set builds momentum with deep improvisations and a climactic closing jam that lifts the entire show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for strong, clear vocals and searing guitar moments; highlighted on Loser, Row Jimmy, Scarlet>Fire and the final Morning Dew jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for an outstanding performance on Broken Arrow and solid support throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart lauded as unsung heroes of the era; long Drums segment in the second set received particular praise.
